title: Body found in river near Brockton water treatment plant, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Body found floating in river near Brockton water treatment plant on Thursday, reported by DPW employees.
Massachusetts State Police retrieved the body, which remains unidentified, for examination by the Chief Medical Examiner.
Plymouth County DA's office has initiated an investigation; past bodies found in 2024 showed no foul play.

title: Man arrested for alleged assault of rideshare passenger at Logan Airport
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Leonard Bacon, 23, of Lowell, arrested for alleged assault at Logan Airport, charged with assault and battery with a dangerous weapon.
Incident occurred at Terminal C; victim reported being stabbed, suffered non-life-threatening injuries before hospital admission.
Bacon slated for Monday court appearance in East Boston District Court; attorney details unavailable as of Sunday.

title: WBZ meteorologist returns after taking 3 years away to care for her son with cancer
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sarah Wroblewski returns to WBZ-TV as a meteorologist after a three-year hiatus to care for her son with cancer.
Her son Declan has been diagnosed with stage 4 medulloblastoma; recent scans show 'no evidence of disease.'
Wroblewski will deliver weekend weather forecasts and some weekday coverage as part of the WBZ Next Weather team.

title: 2 killed in crash on Route 6 near Westport-Dartmouth town line
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two people died in a two-vehicle crash on Route 6 near the Westport-Dartmouth town line Friday evening.
Tristan Bedient and Kate Aldrich were identified as the victims; their Camry sustained catastrophic damage in the collision.
The crash is under investigation by Massachusetts State Police, Dartmouth Police, and Westport Police.

title: Bus driver strike ends in Merrimack Valley after nearly 2 weeks
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Merrimack Valley Transit workers reached a one-year contract agreement, ending a 10-day strike that began on July 1.
The one-year agreement includes a $4 hourly wage increase and preserves Saturday overtime for bus drivers and mechanics.
Regular bus service resumed after the strike, which affected fare-free routes in towns including Lawrence, Haverhill, and Amesbury.

title: 5 takeaways as red-hot Red Sox rally past Mets, 3-2, for 9th straight victory
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox beat Mets 3-2 in extra innings, continuing a nine-game winning streak into the All-Star break.
Boston's bullpen, featuring Aroldis Chapman and Garrett Whitlock, proves crucial in sealing the win against the Mets.
Red Sox rally against Mets marks their second nine-game road trip sweep in franchise history, first since 1977.

title: Noah Kahan joins Fenway Music Hall of Fame after historic four-night run
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Noah Kahan was inducted into the Fenway Music Hall of Fame after his historic four-night run at Fenway Park.
Kahan became the fourth artist honored, joining Billy Joel, Paul McCartney, and the Zac Brown Band as Hall of Famers.
Kahan's shows featured guest appearances from Boston sports figures, including Rob Gronkowski and David Ortiz.

title: AG: Mass. to receive $730,000 from Cash App in alleged deception settlement
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts will receive $730,000 from a $45 million settlement with Cash App over deceptive practices by Block Inc.
Cash App allegedly misled users about safety, failed to warn about rising fraud, and lacked adequate customer support.
The settlement mandates improvements in fraud protection, truthful advertising, and enhanced user support for account issues.

title: Tyngsborough man may face charges after allegedly taking police boat, posing as officer
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 63-year-old Tyngsborough man allegedly impersonated a police officer by using a town police boat on Lake Mascuppic.
The man activated the boat's emergency lights and attempted to enforce boating bylaws, telling people to leave the lake.
Authorities plan to issue a summons for impersonating a police officer; the incident is under investigation by local and state officials.

title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 60 tall ships and military vessels from 20 countries participated in Boston's Parade of Sail event on Saturday.
The USS Constitution led the fleet into Boston Harbor, marking the final stop of the Sail250 tour.
Thousands of spectators gathered along Boston's waterfront to witness the maritime spectacle and ship displays.
